title: Ignacio Allende

infobox.value: (1769-01-21)January 21, 1769  San Miguel el Grande, [Viceroyalty of New Spain](/wiki/Viceroyalty_of_New_Spain "Viceroyalty of New Spain") (now [San Miguel de Allende](/wiki/San_Miguel_de_Allende "San Miguel de Allende"), [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato"), Mexico)

June 26, 1811(1811-06-26) (aged 42)  [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua_City "Chihuahua City"), [Nueva Vizcaya](/wiki/Nueva_Vizcaya,_New_Spain "Nueva Vizcaya, New Spain"), Viceroyalty of New Spain (now [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua_(state) "Chihuahua (state)"), Mexico)

* [Mexican War of Independence](/wiki/Mexican_War_of_Independence "Mexican War of Independence")   + [Capture of Alhóndiga de Granaditas](/wiki/Capture_of_Alh%C3%B3ndiga_de_Granaditas "Capture of Alhóndiga de Granaditas")   + [Battle of Monte de las Cruces](/wiki/Battle_of_Monte_de_las_Cruces "Battle of Monte de las Cruces")   + [Battle of Calderón Bridge](/wiki/Battle_of_Calder%C3%B3n_Bridge "Battle of Calderón Bridge")

infobox.key: Ignacio José de Allende y Unzaga
infobox.value: ([US](/wiki/American_English "American English"): [/ɑːˈjɛndeɪ, -di/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"),[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) [UK](/wiki/British_English "British English"): [/æˈ-, aɪˈɛn-/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"),[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) Spanish: [[iɣˈnasjo aˈʝende]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish"); January 21, 1769 – June 26, 1811), commonly known as **Ignacio Allende**, was a captain of the Spanish Army in [New Spain](/wiki/New_Spain "New Spain") who came to sympathize with the [Mexican independence movement](/wiki/Mexican_War_of_Independence "Mexican War of Independence"). He attended the secret meetings organized by [Josefa Ortiz de Domínguez](/wiki/Josefa_Ortiz_de_Dom%C3%ADnguez "Josefa Ortiz de Domínguez"), where the possibility of an independent [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") was discussed. He fought along with [Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla](/wiki/Miguel_Hidalgo_y_Costilla "Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla") in the first stage of the struggle, eventually succeeding him in leadership of the rebellion. Allende was captured by Spanish colonial authorities while he was in [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila") and executed for [treason](/wiki/Treason "Treason") in [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua_(state) "Chihuahua (state)").

["Allende, Salvador"](https://web.archive.org/web/20210807072602/https://www.lexico.com/definition/allende,_salvador). *[Lexico](/wiki/Lexico "Lexico") UK English Dictionary*. [Oxford University Press](/wiki/Oxford_University_Press "Oxford University Press"). Archived from [the original](http://www.lexico.com/definition/Allende,+Salvador) on 2021-08-07.

["Allende, Isabel"](https://www.ldoceonline.com/dictionary/isabel-allende). *[Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English](/wiki/Longman_Dictionary_of_Contemporary_English "Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English")*. [Longman](/wiki/Longman "Longman"). Retrieved 20 August 2019.

Bancroft, Hubert Howe (1885). ["History of the Pacific States of North America: Mexico. 1883-88"](https://books.google.com/books?id=hdE1AAAAIAAJ&dq=Domingo+Narciso+de+Allende%C2%A0&pg=PA104). Retrieved 5 October 2025.

["Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla"](https://web.archive.org/web/20081121205850/http://www.mexicodesconocido.com.mx/notas/4182-Miguel-Hidalgo-y-Costilla). *Mexico Desconocido* (in Spanish). Mexico City: Grupo Editorial Impresiones Aéreas. Archived from [the original](http://www.mexicodesconocido.com.mx/notas/4182-Miguel-Hidalgo-y-Costilla) on November 21, 2008. Retrieved November 27, 2008.

["¿Quien fue Hidalgo?"](https://web.archive.org/web/20080921041309/http://www.inah.gob.mx/Ninos/miguelhidalgo/index.html) (in Spanish). Mexico: INAH. Archived from [the original](http://www.inah.gob.mx/Ninos/miguelhidalgo/index.html) on September 21, 2008. Retrieved November 27, 2009.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mexican independence leader

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Allende* and the second or maternal family name is  *Unzaga*.

| Ignacio Allende | |
| --- | --- |
| Portrait by Ramón Pérez, 1865 | |
| Born | (1769-01-21)January 21, 1769  San Miguel el Grande, [Viceroyalty of New Spain](/wiki/Viceroyalty_of_New_Spain "Viceroyalty of New Spain") (now [San Miguel de Allende](/wiki/San_Miguel_de_Allende "San Miguel de Allende"), [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato"), Mexico) |
| Died | June 26, 1811(1811-06-26) (aged 42)  [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua_City "Chihuahua City"), [Nueva Vizcaya](/wiki/Nueva_Vizcaya,_New_Spain "Nueva Vizcaya, New Spain"), Viceroyalty of New Spain (now [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua_(state) "Chihuahua (state)"), Mexico) |
| Allegiance | [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain") [Kingdom of Spain](/wiki/History_of_Spain_(1808%E2%80%931874) "History of Spain (1808–1874)")  [Mexican Insurgency](/wiki/Mexican_Army#Independence "Mexican Army") |
| Service years | 1802–1811 |
| Rank | [Captain](/wiki/Captain_(armed_forces) "Captain (armed forces)") (Spain) [Lieutenant General](/wiki/Lieutenant_general "Lieutenant general") (Insurgency) |
| Conflicts | * [Mexican War of Independence](/wiki/Mexican_War_of_Independence "Mexican War of Independence")   + [Capture of Alhóndiga de Granaditas](/wiki/Capture_of_Alh%C3%B3ndiga_de_Granaditas "Capture of Alhóndiga de Granaditas")   + [Battle of Monte de las Cruces](/wiki/Battle_of_Monte_de_las_Cruces "Battle of Monte de las Cruces")   + [Battle of Calderón Bridge](/wiki/Battle_of_Calder%C3%B3n_Bridge "Battle of Calderón Bridge") |

**Ignacio José de Allende y Unzaga** ([US](/wiki/American_English "American English"): [/ɑːˈjɛndeɪ, -di/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"),[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) [UK](/wiki/British_English "British English"): [/æˈ-, aɪˈɛn-/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"),[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) Spanish: [[iɣˈnasjo aˈʝende]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish"); January 21, 1769 – June 26, 1811), commonly known as **Ignacio Allende**, was a captain of the Spanish Army in [New Spain](/wiki/New_Spain "New Spain") who came to sympathize with the [Mexican independence movement](/wiki/Mexican_War_of_Independence "Mexican War of Independence"). He attended the secret meetings organized by [Josefa Ortiz de Domínguez](/wiki/Josefa_Ortiz_de_Dom%C3%ADnguez "Josefa Ortiz de Domínguez"), where the possibility of an independent [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") was discussed. He fought along with [Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla](/wiki/Miguel_Hidalgo_y_Costilla "Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla") in the first stage of the struggle, eventually succeeding him in leadership of the rebellion. Allende was captured by Spanish colonial authorities while he was in [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila") and executed for [treason](/wiki/Treason "Treason") in [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua_(state) "Chihuahua (state)").

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ignacio_Allende&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Ignacio_Allende_statue.jpg/120px-Ignacio_Allende_statue.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ignacio_Allende_statue.jpg)

Allende statue in Chihuahua City, Chihuahua.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/Ignacio_Allende_by_Jose_Ines_Tovilla.jpg/250px-Ignacio_Allende_by_Jose_Ines_Tovilla.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ignacio_Allende_by_Jose_Ines_Tovilla.jpg)

Posthumous portrait of Ignacio Allende (1769–1811).

Allende was born on January 21, 1769, to a wealthy Spanish [criollo](/wiki/Criollo_(people) "Criollo (people)") family in [San Miguel el Grande](/wiki/San_Miguel_de_Allende "San Miguel de Allende") in [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato"), Mexico. His father was Domingo Narciso de Allende y Ayerdi from Spain and a wealthy estate owner and trader.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

In 1802, he joined the army, serving under general [Félix María Calleja](/wiki/F%C3%A9lix_Mar%C3%ADa_Calleja_del_Rey,_1st_Count_of_Calder%C3%B3n "Félix María Calleja del Rey, 1st Count of Calderón"). In 1806, he started to favor the possibility of independence from [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain").
His attendance at a conspiratorial meeting in Valladolid (today [Morelia](/wiki/Morelia "Morelia")) was discovered, in 1809, by the Spanish and went unsanctioned. Regardless, Allende kept supporting the underground independence movement. He was eventually invited by the mayor of [Querétaro](/wiki/Quer%C3%A9taro,_Quer%C3%A9taro "Querétaro, Querétaro"), [Miguel Domínguez](/wiki/Miguel_Dom%C3%ADnguez "Miguel Domínguez") and his wife Josefa Ortíz de Domínguez to discuss further plans for independence at their home. It was during one of these meetings where Allende met Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla and his captain [Juan Aldama](/wiki/Juan_Aldama "Juan Aldama").

Originally, the independence movement was to be led jointly by Allende and Aldama. A change of plans prompted by the discovery of the conspiracy forced Hidalgo to start the rebellion earlier than agreed. The "[Grito de Dolores](/wiki/Grito_de_Dolores "Grito de Dolores")" uttered by Hidalgo signaled the beginning of the revolution, after which the conspirators rallied behind him. The rebel army quickly captured the town of [Dolores](/wiki/Dolores_Hidalgo "Dolores Hidalgo") and marched towards San Miguel el Grande, where Allende obtained the support of his cavalry regiment. On September 22, 1810, Hidalgo y Costilla was officially made [captain general](/wiki/Captain_general "Captain general") of the Revolutionary army while Allende was made [lieutenant general](/wiki/Lieutenant_general "Lieutenant general").
After the famous [capture](/wiki/Capture_of_Alh%C3%B3ndiga_de_Granaditas "Capture of Alhóndiga de Granaditas") of the [Alhóndiga de Granaditas](/wiki/Alh%C3%B3ndiga_de_Granaditas "Alhóndiga de Granaditas"), in Guanajuato, and his victory in the [Battle of Monte de las Cruces](/wiki/Battle_of_Monte_de_las_Cruces "Battle of Monte de las Cruces") Allende suggested Hidalgo march toward [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City") and capture it.
As a consequence of the rebels' defeat in the [Battle of Calderón Bridge](/wiki/Battle_of_Calder%C3%B3n_Bridge "Battle of Calderón Bridge"), the leadership of the Revolutionary army demanded the replacement of Hidalgo as their leader. Allende took this new responsibility and, with a decimated army, he decided to march north to the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), with the goal of making connections with those in the United States for support.[[6]](#cite_note-mexdes-6)[[7]](#cite_note-inah-7)
The rebels, however, were ambushed at the [Wells of Baján](/wiki/Wells_of_Baj%C3%A1n "Wells of Baján") due to the betrayal of [Ignacio Elizondo](/wiki/Ignacio_Elizondo "Ignacio Elizondo"), leading to the capture of Allende, Hidalgo, and several other commanders. Allende's illegitimate child Indalecio was killed during this ambush.

Allende was taken to the city of [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua,_Chihuahua "Chihuahua, Chihuahua") where he was tried for insubordination and imprisoned. He was informed of 2,000 Americans located near [San Antonio de Bexar](/wiki/San_Antonio_de_Bexar "San Antonio de Bexar") and asked for a meeting with the viceroy in order to contemplate a joint solution in defending a potential American invasion. His request was denied and was executed by firing squad on June 26, 1811. His body was decapitated and his head taken to the [Alhóndiga de Granaditas](/wiki/Alh%C3%B3ndiga_de_Granaditas "Alhóndiga de Granaditas") where it was shown to the public inside a cage hung from one corner of the building. In 1824 his remains were buried in the vault reserved for the viceroys and presidents in the cathedral of Mexico. His remains were moved in 1925 to the [Independence Column](/wiki/El_%C3%81ngel "El Ángel") in Mexico City.

## Legacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ignacio_Allende&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Legacy")]

Allende is a national [hero](/wiki/Hero "Hero") of Mexico. Places named in his honor include the municipal district and city of [San Miguel de Allende](/wiki/San_Miguel_de_Allende "San Miguel de Allende") in the state of [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato"), the municipal district of [Allende, Chihuahua](/wiki/Allende,_Chihuahua "Allende, Chihuahua"), the municipality of [Talpa de Allende](/wiki/Talpa_de_Allende "Talpa de Allende") in [Jalisco](/wiki/Jalisco "Jalisco"), [Allende, Nuevo León](/wiki/Allende,_Nuevo_Le%C3%B3n "Allende, Nuevo León"), [Allende, Coahuila](/wiki/Allende,_Coahuila "Allende, Coahuila"), and [Allende metro station](/wiki/Allende_metro_station "Allende metro station"), in Mexico City.
